hours_formula_detail: Florida applies the flat federal ABAWD work standard: 80 hours per month (averaging 20 hours/week). Unpaid volunteering at a nonprofit counts toward those 80 hours as a qualifying work activity — it is NOT an allotment-divided-by-minimum-wage workfare formula for general volunteering. The Florida DCF ABAWD FAQ states ABAWDs must meet the requirement by 'Working at least 80 hours a month (may include volunteering); Participating in employment and training at least 80 hours a month (i.e., SNAP E&T); or Participating in a combination of work and employment and training program for at least 80 hours a month.' Florida Policy Institute's recipient FAQ and the new-law explainer both confirm 'volunteering with a non-profit organization for at least 80 hours a month' satisfies the requirement, and 'working at least 80 hours a month' is the threshold. No Florida primary source uses a SNAP-allotment/minimum-wage hour formula for the general volunteer lane. (Search results that surfaced an 'allotment ÷ minimum wage' workfare formula traced to California/CalFresh county handbooks and generic federal-workfare descriptions, NOT Florida policy.) OBBBA/HR1 (P.L. 119-21, 2025) did not change the 80-hour figure — it expanded the subject population (age 18-64, dependent exemption only if youngest child under 14, removal of veteran/homeless/former-foster exemptions) and curtailed area waivers.
minimum_wage_detail: Not used in the hours computation because Florida uses the flat 80-hour standard, not an allotment/minimum-wage formula for general community service. Per instructions, minimumWageUsd is set to 0 for a federal_80 classification. For the record, Florida's voter-approved (Amendment 2) minimum wage is $14.00/hr through September 29, 2026 and rises to $15.00/hr on September 30, 2026 (tipped cash wage $10.98 → $11.98). Federal floor is $7.25. These wages would only matter if Florida operated an allotment-based workfare formula, which it does not for the general volunteer lane.
qualifying_org_definition: Nonprofit organizations (including 501(c)(3)) qualify as worksites under Florida's SNAP E&T Work Activity / work-experience component (FL FY26 SNAP E&T State Plan). DCF's ABAWD FAQ and Florida Policy Institute confirm 'volunteering with a non-profit organization for at least 80 hours a month' satisfies the ABAWD work requirement. However, for a participant placed into work experience through E&T, the nonprofit worksite must have a signed worksite agreement (MOU) executed by the Local Workforce Development Board (LWDB) before the participant can be assigned there; a participant may identify a nonprofit but cannot begin the placement until the LWDB executes the agreement. Self-directed volunteering reported as a 'work' activity (vs. an E&T work-experience assignment) is treated under the work threshold; whether DCF will accept self-initiated nonprofit volunteer hours entirely outside an E&T assignment is the chief operational uncertainty.
qualifyingOrgShort: A nonprofit (including 501(c)(3)) whose volunteer work counts toward the 80-hour ABAWD requirement; if the placement is made through SNAP E&T work experience, the worksite must have a signed worksite agreement (MOU) executed by the Local Workforce Development Board.
verification_instrument: No named Florida DCF form is dedicated to nonprofit volunteer-hour certification. DCF's official Economic Self Sufficiency (ESS) forms list distinct items: 'Food Stamp Work Registration Notice' (Form ID 256, the CF-ES 2095 work-registration notice — not a volunteer verification), a 'Work Calendar (to report self-employment income)' (Form ID 348 — for self-employment, NOT general volunteer hours), and a 'Work Activity Referral' (Form ID 307). Recipients prove hours by uploading documentation (timesheets, attendance records, a letter from the volunteer organization, or a phone confirmation) to their My ACCESS account, or — for E&T participants — via the regional CareerSource 'Community Service & Work Experience Timesheet' entered into FloridaCommerce's OSST case-management system by career-center staff. Documentation may be submitted in person or virtually (email, fax, or document-management upload).
requiredPrestep: If hours are credited through SNAP E&T work experience: (a) the participant must complete the mandatory E&T orientation/assessment within ~7-10 days of DCF referral, job-search modules, and an Initial Engagement Session within 30 days where activities are assigned, and (b) the nonprofit worksite must have a signed worksite agreement (MOU) executed by the LWDB before the participant can be placed. Whether a participant already meeting 80 hours of self-directed nonprofit volunteering can bypass the E&T assignment ('if assigned' language in the DCF FAQ) is not definitively confirmed by any Florida policy manual. Net required_prestep = 'MOU' (worksite agreement) plus the E&T orientation/assignment sequence.
remote_stance_detail: CareerSource policy WT073 allows some E&T components (e.g., supervised job search) to be conducted remotely, but work experience / Work Activity placements at nonprofit worksites — the component through which nonprofit volunteer hours are credited — are physical, on-site, employer-supervised placements. No Florida source describes remote/virtual volunteering at a nonprofit as a qualifying work-experience activity. For the high-stakes 'remoteStance' enum, remote volunteering through the credited lane is therefore recorded as 'disallowed' (narrative tier: 'restricted' — remote is allowed for non-credit-bearing E&T components only).
accepts_outofstate_detail: No Florida DCF policy, FY26 State Plan, or CareerSource policy (including WT073) addresses whether the certifying/supervising nonprofit must be incorporated or located in Florida. The requirement is that the worksite hold a signed MOU/worksite agreement executed by the local LWDB — the nonprofit's state of legal registration is not addressed. Under the federal default (7 CFR 273.24) any qualifying nonprofit may serve as a worksite; absent an explicit state restriction an out-of-state 501(c)(3) operating a worksite in Florida and executing an LWDB worksite agreement should be acceptable, but no source affirmatively states this — hence 'silent'. The MOU requirement, not org domicile, is the real operational barrier.
geographic_waivers: None — fully enforced statewide in FY2026. Florida has no active ABAWD time-limit waivers (Oct 2025-Sep 2026). OBBBA/HR1 (signed July 4, 2025) removed the provision under which prior area waivers were granted; all prior waivers expired before, or were terminated by USDA on, November 2, 2026, and as of February 2026 USDA listed no active ABAWD time-limit waivers nationwide — 'the time limit should be enforced everywhere throughout the country.' Florida specifically 'does not have a waiver.' The elimination of county-specific waivers means all Florida counties — including rural areas that may previously have qualified on unemployment grounds — now have the time limit enforced uniformly statewide.
geographicWaivers: None active in FY2026. Florida is fully enforced statewide with zero geographic/county ABAWD waivers; OBBBA (2025) eliminated the area-waiver authority and prior waivers expired/were terminated by USDA. A secondary blog (benefitsusa.org) listing Florida as 'Partial | Some rural counties qualify' reflects stale pre-OBBBA framing and is contradicted by USDA and multiple FY2026 sources.
submissionMechanism: Recipients upload documentation (timesheets, org letter, attendance records) to the My ACCESS portal (myaccess.myflfamilies.com) using the document-upload feature, or submit to DCF/CareerSource by email, fax, or in person. E&T participants submit the CareerSource 'Community Service & Work Experience Timesheet' to their assigned Career Navigator, entered into OSST. Reportable ABAWD changes (hours falling below 80/month) can also be reported via My ACCESS 'Report a Change' or by phone (850-300-4323).
reportingCadence: Monthly. ABAWDs must report when work/volunteer hours fall below 80 in a month. Regional CareerSource offices set their own timesheet cadence — e.g., Capital Region requires submission by the 15th and 30th of each month; some other regions require weekly submission. No single statewide deadline.
submission_deadline: Varies by CareerSource region. CareerSource Capital Region: timesheets/job-search logs 'should be submitted by the 15th and 30th to ensure sufficient time to update the system and reflect recorded hours.' Some regions (e.g., Brevard/Flagler/Volusia) require weekly submission. No single statewide deadline — participants follow their assigned career center's schedule. ABAWD compliance is assessed monthly.
submissionDeadline: No single statewide deadline. Region-dependent: CareerSource Capital Region requires timesheets by the 15th and 30th of each month; some regions require weekly submission. Compliance is assessed monthly; report drops below 80 hrs/month promptly via My ACCESS or to the assigned career center.
notes:
VERIFICATION PASS (2026-06-22, FY2026/OBBBA): Confirmed the prior HIGH-confidence finding and made two corrections. (1) CLASSIFICATION = federal_80 — affirmatively confirmed. Florida applies the flat 80-hour/month (20 hr/wk) standard; unpaid nonprofit volunteering counts toward those 80 hours, NOT via an allotment/minimum-wage workfare formula. DCF ABAWD FAQ verbatim: meet the requirement by 'Working at least 80 hours a month (may include volunteering)... or Participating in a combination of work and employment and training program for at least 80 hours a month.' Florida Policy Institute (new-law explainer + recipient FAQ): 'working at least 80 hours a month' and 'volunteering with a non-profit organization for at least 80 hours a month' satisfy the requirement. CAUTION DOCUMENTED: WebSearch repeatedly surfaced an 'allotment ÷ minimum wage' workfare-hours formula, but on inspection every instance traced to California/CalFresh county handbooks (e.g., Santa Clara County) or generic federal-workfare descriptions — NOT Florida policy. Do not misattribute that formula to Florida. (2) ENFORCEMENT/WAIVERS = statewide, none. USDA: after OBBBA the area-waiver authority was removed; all prior waivers expired before / were terminated by Nov 2, 2026; as of Feb 2026 USDA listed no active waivers and 'the time limit should be enforced everywhere'; Florida 'does not have a waiver.' This contradicts a benefitsusa.org blog that lists FL as 'Partial | Some rural counties qualify' — that is stale pre-OBBBA framing and is rejected.

CORRECTION to prior verification_instrument claim: A secondary blog (bsimbframes.com) asserted a 'CF-ES 3007 Work Calendar' is the monthly volunteer-hours log. The OFFICIAL DCF ESS forms page does NOT support this: it lists 'Work Calendar (to report self-employment income)' (Form ID 348, scoped to self-employment), 'Food Stamp Work Registration Notice' (Form ID 256 = the CF-ES 2095 registration notice), and 'Work Activity Referral' (Form ID 307). None is a dedicated nonprofit volunteer-hour certification form. verificationFormName is therefore 'unknown'; the de-facto instruments are My ACCESS document upload, an org letter/timesheet, or the regional CareerSource 'Community Service & Work Experience Timesheet' entered into OSST.

FL minimum wage captured for context only (federal_80 → minimumWageUsd=0): $14.00/hr through Sep 29, 2026; $15.00/hr from Sep 30, 2026. FY2026 SNAP max allotment for a 1-person household = $298 (48 states, eff. Oct 1, 2025) — recorded only to illustrate that no allotment-based formula is in play.

remoteStance recorded as 'disallowed' for the credited lane: per WT073, supervised job search may be remote, but the nonprofit work-experience component (the lane that credits volunteer hours) is a physical on-site placement; no source describes remote nonprofit volunteering as qualifying. countyVariation = false at the policy level (state-administered, uniform 80-hr rule), though CareerSource regions vary in timesheet cadence/deadlines (operational, not eligibility-determining).

--- PRIOR-PASS HISTORY (preserved from states/FL.json) ---
Florida explicitly allows volunteer hours at nonprofits (80 hrs/month) to satisfy the ABAWD work requirement and participant self-reporting is accepted in many cases, so the general volunteer lane exists. Florida operates a mandatory SNAP E&T program: DCF refers work-requirement participants to FloridaCommerce/CareerSource, which assesses participants and assigns at least one E&T activity; orientation/assessment is required at minimum. No named Florida-specific DCF form for nonprofit volunteer hour certification was identified (CF-ES-2095 is a work-registration notice).
FINAL PASS (2026-06-21): Extracted full text of FL FY26 SNAP E&T State Plan (approved 9/22/2025) and CareerSource WT073 (eff. 8/21/2025). State Plan: 'LWDBs arrange worksites within the private for-profit sector, the non-profit sector, and the public sector'; a participant 'will not be assigned to work experience at the worksite until an agreement with the site has been executed by the LWDB' — making MOU the accurate required_prestep. The plan lists no standalone 'Community Service'/'Volunteer' component; nonprofit volunteering is realized through the Work Activity component with LWDB-brokered worksite agreements. WT073: orientation is a hard required activity within 7 days of referral; an Individual Employment Plan must be developed at the initial engagement appointment before any component assignment. The 'meets work requirements on their own' language appears only in the provider-determination context, not as a pre-E&T bypass for self-directed volunteers. Remote: supervised job search may be remote, work experience is on-site. Verification: case files include timesheets/attendance/check stubs in OSST; documentation may be submitted in person or virtually. Confidence elevated to 'high'.
EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): hours formula = federal_80; out-of-state nonprofit = silent; geographic waivers = none statewide; submission via email/fax/in-person/document upload, monthly with regional variation; form fields derived from WT073 and CareerSource timesheet references (no notarization or EIN required per any FL source).
conflicts_with_prior: verification_instrument: REJECTED the secondary-source claim (bsimbframes.com) that 'CF-ES 3007 Work Calendar' is the volunteer-hours log. The official DCF ESS forms page shows the 'Work Calendar' (Form ID 348) is scoped to self-employment income, not general volunteer hours; no dedicated volunteer-certification form exists. verificationFormName set to 'unknown' (consistent with prior file's underlying finding).

geographic waivers: a secondary aggregator (benefitsusa.org) listing Florida as 'Partial | Some rural counties qualify' was checked and REJECTED as stale pre-OBBBA framing; USDA and FY2026 sources confirm no active Florida waivers — affirming the prior file's 'None statewide' finding against the contradicting blog.

Classification surfaced explicitly as the high-stakes 'classification':'federal_80' (prior file carried hours_formula='federal_80' but not the dedicated 'classification' field). Affirmatively confirmed FL does NOT use an allotment/minimum-wage formula for general nonprofit volunteering.

remoteStance normalized to the high-stakes enum 'disallowed' for the credited (work-experience) lane; prior file used the narrative value 'restricted' (retained in remote_stance). Same underlying finding.
unresolved: Whether a recipient who is ALREADY self-directing 80 hours/month of nonprofit volunteer work can have those hours counted WITHOUT going through the mandatory E&T orientation + LWDB-brokered work-experience assignment (the DCF FAQ's 'if assigned' language) is not definitively resolved by any Florida policy manual or the FY26 State Plan. This is the single biggest operational uncertainty for a platform like Tended.

No named org-facing Florida DCF form for nonprofit volunteer-hour certification exists; documentation is an org letter/timesheet or the regional CareerSource 'Community Service & Work Experience Timesheet' (entered into OSST). verificationFormName = 'unknown'.

Out-of-state 501(c)(3) acceptance is not affirmatively stated; policy is silent and would default to federal rules. The LWDB worksite-agreement (MOU) requirement, not org domicile, is the binding constraint.

Remote/virtual nonprofit volunteering is not described as a qualifying work-experience activity; recorded as 'disallowed' for the credited lane, but no source explicitly forbids it in writing — it is simply not provided for.

The USDA FNA FY2025-2029 waiver page and the FY26 State Plan PDF intermittently returned timeouts/403 this pass; FL waiver status and the MOU/state-plan findings were corroborated via readable secondary sources and prior-pass primary extractions.
